identity.name: The Miller Who Never Paid
identity.kind: miller, retired (the debt outlived the mill)
identity.story: Shorted a piper's fee once, in a town two tales over — these things get paid for eventually, whether or not you're the one who owes them. Settled in Fableton and found only one piper left to pay: a cousin of that one, working strictly rats, strictly contracted. Neither has ever agreed whose debt it actually is, and neither has ever let that stop the payments.

relationships.kind: pays down a fee that was never his cousin's to begin with, one short coin at a time

child.label: grinding a morning's flour, short the same coin as always
paying down what was never quite his to owe

children.label: crossing to where the lesser piper keeps his rounds
handing over a coin the piper cousin didn't ask for and won't refuse

child.label: sweeping the mill floor, keeping better count than he ever did the first time
sleeping easy, one coin lighter than a debt that isn't his
